source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The rectangle shown in the diagram on the right is divided into 7 squares. The sides of the grey squares on the right are all $8 \mathrm{~cm}$ long. What is the length in $\mathrm{cm}$ of the side of the black square? <image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'shown in the diagram'
the unknown side length depends on the arrangement of 7 squares and the location of the grey squares on the right, which is not fully specified in text alone
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and one image asset is present
multi_step: the task is not direct lookup; it asks for an unknown square side from a rectangle partitioned into 7 squares
the text implies combining multiple geometric constraints: equal side lengths for the grey squares, rectangle partitioning, and the black square size
answer_type is numeric, suggesting a determinate calculation rather than open-ended description
metadata.multi_solution_policy.should_push_multi_solution_agent=true indicates upstream belief that the item has meaningful solution-structure potential
verifiability: answer_text='18' is a clear numeric target
a geometric derivation from the diagram can in principle be checked against the numeric result

decision_reasons: question, image, and numeric answer are all present, so the core semantic assets are intact
registry passed with no recorded asset issues
high image dependency is adequately supported because the required diagram asset exists
verifiability is strong because the target answer is a clear numeric value
although the layout is not fully recoverable from text alone, this is not a blocker since the image is available
